content.text:
<user_query>
make the "Designed to elevate your print brand." homepage component 50% shorter. Also, the button and the badge are full width and they neew to be hug
</user_query>

Searching the codebase for the homepage component that contains that copy.

[REDACTED]

input.pattern: Designed to elevate your print brand

content.text:
Halving the intro feature-split from 480px to 240px and fixing grid stretch so the pill and button hug their content.

[REDACTED]

Halving the intro section height (480px → 240px) and fixing grid stretch: grid items default to `justify-items: stretch`, so we'll set `width: fit-content` (and `max-width: 100%`) on the pill and button. Adding slightly tighter intro-specific spacing so content fits the shorter block.

[REDACTED]

Removing the button's extra top margin in this section — grid `gap` already spaces items.

[REDACTED]

Here’s what changed:

### 1. **Section ~50% shorter**
`.printonet-feature-split--intro` was **480px** tall (including media/body rows). It’s now **240px** (`min-height`, `height`, `grid-template-rows`, and the media/body height rules).

To keep the copy readable in a shorter band, **intro-only** tweaks were added: slightly smaller **h2** / **p**, tighter **padding** and **gap**, and **`margin-top: 0`** on the CTA (spacing comes from the grid **gap** instead of the global `.printonet-btn` top margin).

### 2. **Pill and button “hug” content**
`.printonet-feature-split__body` is a **grid**, and the default is **`justify-items: stretch`**, so children (including the pill and link) were stretching to the full column width.

Updates in `style.css`:

- **`justify-items: start`** on `.printonet-feature-split__body` so items align to the start.
- **`h2` and `p`** get **`width: 100%`** and **`max-width: 48ch`** so the heading and paragraph still use a normal text column width.
- **`.printonet-pill`** and **`.printonet-btn`** inside the body get **`width: fit-content`**, **`max-width: 100%`**, and **`justify-self: start`** so they only span their content (and stay usable on narrow screens).

Edits live in `/root/printonet-multitenant/wp-content/themes/printonet-theme/style.css` — the **Creator Spotlight** feature split uses the same body classes and gets the same hugging behavior; only **`--intro`** sets the shorter **240px** height.

[REDACTED]
